item.title: Key spy power on verge of lapsing after Trump appoints controversial intel chief
item.description: Democrats say they won't vote to reauthorize the key spy authority known as FISA Section 702 as long as Bill Pulte is leading the Office of the Director of National Intelligence.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony found guilty of murder in stabbing of Austin Metcalf
item.description: The trial of Karmelo Anthony centered on whether he acted in self-defense when he fatally stabbed Austin Metcalf at a Frisco ISD track meet in 2025.

item.title: Details on Karmelo Anthony's sentencing after murder conviction for Texas track meet stabbing
item.description: A jury on Tuesday found Karmelo Anthony, 19, guilty of murder for the 2025 stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a Texas track meet. CBS News correspondent Jonah Kaplan reports.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony convicted of murder in 2025 stabbing of Austin Metcalf at Texas track meet
item.description: Karmelo Anthony was convicted of murder Tuesday for the 2025 stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf at a Texas track meet. CBS News Texas reports.

item.title: May inflation likely topped 4% for first time in 3 years, economists say
item.description: Economists expect the Consumer Price Index this week to show U.S. inflation continuing to rise due to higher energy costs.

item.title: Vance: Iran deal could happen in a week or months from now
item.description: In an interview airing on "CBS Sunday Morning," Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. will reach a deal with Iran before November's midterm elections.

item.title: Vance says U.S. "very close" to deal with Iran, could come in a week or months
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. is "very close" to reaching a deal with Iran, but it could come next week or months from now. "I think that the deal could happen in the next week, but the deal could also happen months from now," Vance told Robert Costa in an interview airing Sunday.

item.title: DOJ finds EEOC violated law by pressuring employers to make race-based decisions
item.description: The Justice Department accused the EEOC of violating civil rights laws by issuing guidelines that effectively pressured employers to make race-based considerations in hiring and promotions.

item.title: How much interest can a $200,000 CD account earn now?
item.description: Moving $200,000 out of your retirement account into a CD may make sense now. Here's how much interest you can earn.

item.title: Dozens of 7,000-year-old headless human skeletons found in ditch
item.description: Researchers have discovered dozens of headless human skeletons in a ditch in Slovakia, which they believe date back 7,000 years.

item.title: Many soccer fans excited about U.S. hosting World Cup, CBS News poll finds
item.description: The U.S. being a host country for the World Cup generates interest among fans, but less so among those who are not soccer fans.

item.title: Mortgage rates dos and don'ts that borrowers should know now
item.description: In today's unique economic climate, borrowers need to understand these four specific mortgage rate dos and don'ts.

item.title: House to vote on ICE funding, ending months-long impasse
item.description: The House will be voting on Republicans' $70 billion bill to fund immigration enforcement agencies for the rest of the Trump administration.

item.title: Trump says Iran shot down Apache helicopter and U.S. must respond
item.description: The two helicopter pilots were uninjured but the U.S. "must, of necessity, respond," President Trump said Tuesday.

item.title: Ukraine winning war with Russia, retired U.S. generals say
item.description: Several retired U.S. generals and the former director of a U.S. intelligence agency told CBS News they believe Ukraine now has the upper hand in the war with Russia.

item.title: Early details on new Artemis III mission
item.description: NASA has announced the four astronauts who will take part in the Artemis III moon mission. Derrick Pitts, chief astronomer for the Franklin Institute, joined CBS News to discuss what we know about the mission so far.

item.title: Suspect in Charlotte train stabbing found incompetent for trial "at this time"
item.description: Decarlos Brown Jr, the man accused of fatally stabbing Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte, North Carolina, light rail train, has been ruled incompetent for trial "at this time." CBS News correspondent Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Here's the retirement challenge nobody talks about
item.description: Many Americans spend decades saving for retirement, but lack a plan for using that money once they stop working, a new survey finds. Here's what to know.

item.title: How to buy SpaceX shares as its blockbuster IPO readies for liftoff
item.description: SpaceX is setting aside a large chunk of shares for ordinary investors as it seeks to raise a record $75 billion. Here's what to know.

item.title: $8,000 CD vs. $8,000 high-yield savings account: Which will earn more interest in one year?
item.description: CD and high-yield savings accounts remain viable tools for savers. Here's how much an $8,000 deposit will earn in each.

item.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
item.description: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

item.title: U.S. Apache helicopter shot down by Iran, Trump says; crew rescued by sea drone
item.description: The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone in the first such operation ever carried out by the U.S. military, officials told CBS News.

item.title: When should you repair an AC unit instead of buying a new one?
item.description: Repairing your AC can save you thousands of dollars, but only if you know when fixing it makes financial sense.

item.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

item.title: Trump says U.S. must respond after Apache helicopter attack over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said on social media that the U.S. "must respond" to the attack on an Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Trump says Iran shot down U.S. Apache helicopter, crew rescued by sea drone
item.description: President Trump is vowing to respond after confirming that Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone, the first rescue of its kind. CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

item.title: Voters across 4 states gear up for key primaries ahead of 2026 midterm elections
item.description: Voters in Maine, South Carolina, Nevada and North Dakota are heading to the polls Tuesday for key primaries, including the race in Maine involving controversial Democratic U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner.

item.title: Platner seeks Democratic nomination to take on Sen. Collins in closely watched race
item.description: Maine Democrat Graham Platner is seeking to make the nomination official to take on Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November.

item.title: Judge voids Trump's $100,000 fee for new H-1B visas
item.description: U.S. District Judge Leo Sorokin ruled in favor of a group of 20 states that challenged President Trump's new $100,000 visa fee.

item.title: Rome mayor resigns over expense-account scandal
item.description: Ignazio Marino says he's being made a scapegoat for rooting out mafia infiltration in city hall, vows to expose the truth
